name: colab-mcp
description: Drive a Google Colab notebook from your AI client: create, edit, run, and read cells, and switch GPU runtimes.
long_description:
An MCP server that bridges your AI client (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) to a live Google Colab notebook in your browser. All nine tools are pre-registered up front, so they appear immediately in clients that ignore `tools/list_changed` (Claude Code, Codex, Kiro). Call `open_colab_browser_connection` once to open a Colab tab and unlock the notebook editing tools. This fork also adds stale-server detection and programmatic GPU control (T4 / L4 / A100).

Requires [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) to be installed (it bootstraps Python 3.13 and all dependencies automatically on first launch).

tools.name: open_colab_browser_connection
tools.description: Open a connection to a Google Colab browser session and unlock the notebook editing tools.

tools.name: add_code_cell
tools.description: Add a new code cell to the Colab notebook.

tools.name: add_text_cell
tools.description: Add a new text/markdown cell to the Colab notebook.

tools.name: get_cells
tools.description: Read the current notebook state: cells with their IDs, contents, and outputs.

tools.name: run_code_cell
tools.description: Execute a code cell in the Colab notebook by cellId.

tools.name: update_cell
tools.description: Update the contents of an existing cell.

tools.name: delete_cell
tools.description: Delete a cell from the Colab notebook by cellId.

tools.name: move_cell
tools.description: Move a cell to a new position in the notebook.

tools.name: change_runtime
tools.description: Change the Colab runtime accelerator (NONE, T4, L4, A100). Requires OAuth setup.
